statement.headerDoc:
Paper 119 — Q7 (Paper 118) empirical census
  ============================================

  Q7 (Paper 118) asked whether every odd integer m with orbit(m) ∋ 1 and
  K(m) = 41 is exactly m = 911.

  ★ FALSIFIED ★ — there are 61 distinct odd m in [1, 10000] with K(m) = 41.
  This file records the explicit list and proves each member satisfies K = 41
  via native_decide.  The 61-element list was generated by
  test/paper119-q7-q8-q9-collatz-analysis.ts.

  We prove:
    (1) Each listed m has stepsTo 1 m 100 = 41.
    (2) The list has exactly 61 elements.
    (3) 135 is in the list and 135 ≠ 911

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Zero-sorry verified by lake env lean
